items.title: Boy charged with murdering mother, attempting to kill sister in Perth

children.content: A 16-year-old boy is charged with murdering his mother and attempting to kill his sister in Perth's south-east, as police say the girl is being treated in hospital for "very serious" stab wounds.

gallery.altText: Police tape off a suburban driveway
Police stand around on a lawn of a house

thumbnail.altText: Police tape off a suburban driveway
Police stand around on a lawn of a house

items.title: WA man dies after not being able to get antipsychotic medication

children.content: Bradley Buswell, aged in his 40s and living with schizophrenia, died in state care after his medication became unavailable due to national shortages. An ongoing coronial inquest is probing the circumstances of his death.

image.altText: Bradley Buswell smiles wearing a collared shirt.

items.title: 'Massive' drop in tourists this winter hits WA's south

children.content: Despite being an idyllic location for a winter getaway, businesses in WA's Great Southern have recorded a decline in tourists this season.

image.altText: A man wearing a cap with ocean in background looking at camera

items.title: States can use their own emergency warnings when AusAlert goes live

children.content: State and territory representatives had voiced significant concerns about the rollout of the national emergency warning system in a meeting with the minister Kristy McBain.

image.altText: A phone notification with the words 'Priority AusAlert'.

items.title: Former Catholic bishop sexually abused boys at 'shirts-off' parties, court hears

children.content: One of Australia's longest serving senior Catholic clerics had a sexual interest in young Aboriginal men and abused them in Western Australia's far north over several years, a Perth court hears.

image.altText: A close-up shot of the former Bishop of Broome, Christopher Alan Saunders, arriving at court in Perth, in front of another man.

items.title: Police probe use of force on handcuffed teen injured during Perth arrest

children.content: A handcuffed teenager was injured when he was forced to the ground by a police officer, sparking a WA Police review after video of the incident emerged.

items.title: 'Don't go there': Hanson fires up in Perth over White Australia policy

children.content: Speaking at a Perth leadership breakfast, Pauline Hanson angrily denies suggestions her strident opposition to migration by anyone from "radical Islamic countries" amounts to a version of the White Australia policy.

articles.title: Who paid for Pauline Hanson's trip to Europe? Not taxpayers, she says

children.content: In her first appearance since returning to Australia, Pauline Hanson fields questions about her trip to Europe with billionaire Gina Rinehart.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson sits on stage talking during a question and answer session at a breakfact function, against a blue background.

gallery.altText: Pauline is pictured at a lectern advertising the Leadership Matters event against a blue background.

thumbnail.altText: Pauline is pictured at a lectern advertising the Leadership Matters event against a blue background.

items.title: Woman's family wrongly told she was dead in hospital identity error

children.content: A Melbourne hospital apologises after mixing up the medical data of two people, resulting in one of them being wrongly declared dead by police.

image.altText: Michelle Buckley stands indoors, dressed in a warm cardigan.

items.title: 'Sit with them in their grief': Supporting someone who has lost an infant

children.content: Women who have lost children talk about continuing in the wake of such grief, and how people stepped up, or stepped away.

image.altText: Ant, Eddie and son Seb take a selfie

items.title: How school is different for Jewish Australian kids

children.content: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion hears from Jewish school leaders about how they need to implement extraordinary and expensive security measures.

image.altText: Rows of empty black desks, each with a white booklet on top and either brown or cream seats in a green room.

items.title: Cameron McEvoy has lived a life between trips to Glasgow

children.content: As one of just two Australian swimmers returning to Glasgow for a second Commonwealth Games, Cameron McEvoy is taking time to appreciate how much has happened to him in the past 12 years.

image.altText: Cameron McEvoy at the Commonwealth Games and Pan Pacs swimming trials.

items.title: Failings behind one of WA's worst sex abuse scandals laid bare by whistleblower

children.content: As one of Western Australia's most monstrous serial paedophiles approaches the end of his prison term, the woman who helped bring his crimes to light is publishing her account of the story.

gallery.altText: Dennis McKenna in brown shirt, tie and jacket on the left. On the right a photo of brick buildings.
A woman sitting in front of a bookcase

thumbnail.altText: Dennis McKenna in brown shirt, tie and jacket on the left. On the right a photo of brick buildings.
A woman sitting in front of a bookcase

items.title: As The Odyssey smashes box office records one name is drawing people in

children.content: The Odyssey's director Sir Christopher Nolan has a stellar catalogue. Interstellar, even. But as his new film smashes box office records is it safe to crown him the new king of Hollywood?

image.altText: Christopher Nolan on set with arm up, Matt Damon and Zendaya in character off to one side

items.title: New Ozploitation horror is like Puberty Blues from hell

children.content: After spending years in special effects and make-up, filmmaker Mia'Kate Russell's ocker nightmare Penny Lane Is Dead has made it to the silver screen.

image.altText: A man and a woman stand on a veranda

items.title: Travel quiz: join us for a trip around the world

children.content: Where are macadamia nuts indigenous to? And what’s the capital city of Switzerland?

image.altText: A scenic shot shows the sloping hills of Norfolk Island

items.title: Albanese's career mirrors Howard's in two important ways

children.content: Anthony Albanese has been in near-fatal vehicle accidents not once, but three times. Politically, his is also a story of survival against the odds.

image.altText: A man and a woman sit eating a meal at a kitchen table

avatar.alt: A close up photo of Annabel Crabb, who has long brown hair and is wearing red spectacles, red lipstick and a blue top

items.title: Map reveals Australia's 'pharmacy deserts' are not all in the bush

children.content: Australia's "pharmacy deserts" have been mapped for the first time, revealing remote NT communities and outer-city Melbourne have fewer pharmacies per person than some other parts of the country.

gallery.altText: A female pharmacist is selecting a drug from the pharmacy inventory.

thumbnail.altText: A female pharmacist is selecting a drug from the pharmacy inventory.

items.title: AI companies 'stole' Australian culture, news bosses say

children.content: The heads of the ABC, SBS, Nine and News Corp say AI companies should strike licensing deals rather than freely train on Australian news, warning journalism is a public good that must be protected.

image.altText: Hamish MacDonald,  Allesandra Galloni, Hugh Marks, Michael Miller, Jane Palfreyman, Matt Stanton.

items.title: Judgment day for Rebel Wilson's messy defamation case

children.content: Hollywood star Rebel Wilson will learn today whether she defamed the lead actor on her "fun girl-power" film, in a judgment to be handed down in the Federal Court.

image.altText: Rebel Wilson outside court wearing a green jacket.

items.title: Gas tax, gambling and AI at stake at the Labor conference

children.content: Labor members and unions are gearing up for fights over gambling, gas and AI at the party's national conference later this week.

items.title: Tony Modra shares post-surgery photos after truck crash

children.content: AFL legend Tony Modra shares a post-crash update on social media, posting photos of him in hospital and with family, and saying he is now "getting out and about".

image.altText: Adelaide Crows legend Tony Modra with family.

items.title: UK YouTuber Yung Filly violently sexually assaulted young fan, court hears

children.content: A Perth court hears disturbing details of the YouTube personality's alleged sexual assault of a young woman who had a "celebrity crush" on him.
